events.title: OpenAI unveils GPT-5.6 AI models with phased rollout and stronger safety checks
events.summary: OpenAI has unveiled GPT-5.6, its latest AI model family with Sol, Terra and Luna. The models will roll out gradually across ChatGPT, the API and Codex, with Sol adding Max and Ultra modes for advanced reasoning.

events.title: OpenAI weighs IPO in 2027 after expected Anthropic public debut
events.summary: AI giant OpenAI is reportedly eyeing a 2027 initial public offering, potentially after rival Anthropic. Both companies have confidentially filed for an IPO, with Anthropic possibly listing as early as October. Recent tech market volatility has influenced OpenAI's timing, thoug…

events.title: Italy regulator probes Microsoft over 'Microsoft 365' price hike
events.summary: Italy's antitrust watchdog has launched a probe into Microsoft for alleged unfair practices concerning its Microsoft 365 subscription price increase. The regulator claims consumers weren't properly notified about the integration of AI tools like Copilot and Designer. Customers…

events.title: OpenAI leans toward waiting until next year for IPO, NYT reports

weekly.intro: 105 AI items this week (71 India-relevant) across 9 sources.

items.title: The fittest founder in the room got cancer. Here’s how he used AI to fight back.
items.summary: When confronted with cancer, Connor Christou fed everything tied tied to his regime — blood results, scan data, wearable output, journal entries — into Claude.

items.title: OpenAI limits GPT-5.6 rollout after government request, says restrictions shouldn’t be the norm
items.summary: “We don’t believe this kind of government access process should become the long-term default,” says OpenAI. “It keeps the best tools from users, developers, enterprises, cyber defenders, and global partners who need them.”

items.title: The White House is asking OpenAI to slow roll the release of its new model over safety concerns
items.summary: OpenAI reportedly plans to share its newest model, GPT 5.6, with a select group of partners instead of with the broader public. The reason: the Trump administration told it to.

items.title: Anthropic’s Claude is winning over paid consumers, a market owned by ChatGPT
items.summary: Despite ChatGPT's commanding market lead, consumers who pay for AI have been increasingly choosing Anthropic's Claude, data shows.

items.title: Apple Vision Pro exec is reportedly leaving for OpenAI
items.summary: Paul Meade, the Apple vice president in charge of the Vision Pro headset, is reportedly leaving the company to join OpenAI’s hardware team.

items.title: General Intuition’s $2.3B bet that video games can train AI agents for the real world
items.summary: General Intuition has raised $320 million to scale AI trained on millions of hours of gameplay, betting action data can help AI develop something closer to human intuition.

items.title: Databricks’ former AI chief thinks he can cut AI’s power bill by 1,000x
items.summary: Un-0 is an image-generation system tool that shows for the first time how the company's technology can replicate conventional AI systems.

items.title: Adobe acquires image and video enhancement tool maker Topaz Labs
items.summary: Adobe said that it will integrate Topaz Labs' tools across its apps.

items.title: Patronus AI lands $50M to build ‘digital worlds’ that stress-test AI agents
items.summary: Agent-testing startup Patronus AI, founded by former Meta AI researchers, is experiencing nearly insatiable demand, its investor says.

careers.note: Demand snapshot for 2026. ML Engineer is the most in-demand AI title; agentic-AI and deep-learning skills carry the highest pay premiums; Python + one cloud is the baseline. Specialisation beats generalisation for freshers.

paths.note: #1 AI title — build, deploy & maintain models in production. Best all-round entry into AI.
RAG, tool-use & multi-agent systems. ~40% of enterprise apps will embed AI agents by year-end.
Neural nets for vision & speech. Highest premiums; usually needs strong maths / grad study.
Ship & scale models on the cloud. Cloud AI certs add 20–25% to salary.
Foundational, widest entry door; a reliable first step into AI.
Imaging, robotics, autonomous systems, medical AI.
Powers chatbots, search & agents — the hottest application area.
5–8 yrs experience; $140k–$332k bands. Translates AI into business value.
Alignment, evals, red-teaming & policy — newer but expanding fast.

fresher.why: The lingua franca of ML — non-negotiable.
Linear algebra, probability & calculus — enough to reason about models.
Regression, classification, evaluation, the ML workflow.
Build & train neural nets; PyTorch dominates research & jobs.
Ship a small LLM app — the most visible skill to recruiters now.
Real AI work is mostly data; SQL is everywhere.
AWS leads (~40% of postings), then Azure & GCP. Version everything.
A deployed project that solves a real problem is the #1 differentiator.

experienced.why: Docker, CI/CD, monitoring, model/versioning — production is where value lives.
Multi-agent orchestration, tool-use, evals — the surging frontier.
AWS/Azure/GCP architecture; certs add a 20–25% salary premium.
Adapt, measure and harden models for a domain.
Cost & latency engineering for large models.
Trade-offs across data, latency, cost & reliability at scale.
CV, NLP, recsys, healthcare — depth beats breadth at senior levels.
Leading teams and tying models to measurable outcomes.

profile.name: Harman Singh Sandhu
profile.blurb: I build AI/ML projects and write about them. This site is one of them — an automated daily AI digest.
